name: trivially_aligned_all
code:
proof fn phi_1_trivially_aligned_all(addr: nat, size: nat)
    requires
        size > 0,
    ensures
        aligned(addr, size),
{
}
reason: If every address is aligned to every nonzero size, the spec is vacuously trivial and useless

name: aligned_zero_size
code:
proof fn phi_2_aligned_zero_size(addr: nat)
    requires
        addr > 0,
    ensures
        aligned(addr, 0),
{
}
reason: Nonzero addresses should not be considered aligned to size 0, which is a degenerate division-by-zero case

name: alignment_survives_increment
code:
proof fn phi_3_alignment_survives_increment(addr: nat, size: nat)
    requires
        size > 1,
        aligned(addr, size),
    ensures
        aligned(addr + 1, size),
{
}
reason: Incrementing an aligned address by 1 should break alignment for any size > 1

name: aligned_odd_to_even
reason: 7 is not a multiple of 4; if the spec says it is aligned, modular arithmetic is broken

name: alignment_implies_double_alignment
code:
proof fn phi_5_alignment_implies_double_alignment(addr: nat, size: nat)
    requires
        size > 0,
        aligned(addr, size),
    ensures
        aligned(addr, 2 * size),
{
}
reason: Being aligned to size does not imply alignment to 2*size (e.g., 4 is aligned to 4 but not to 8)
